name: Free Build
concept: Make your own levels — then dare someone to beat them

learn: You've been solving other people's puzzles. Now YOU make them
Paint walls, drop gems, place your hero and the flag — it's your world
Every level you save goes in the Workshop for anyone on this iPad to play
Making a puzzle is harder than solving one — you have to know the answer AND make the question fun
That's real game design. It's the same job the people who made your favorite games do

blocks.name: the paint tools
blocks.text: tap a tool, then tap the grid. Walls, floor, gems, the flag, and your hero

blocks.name: hero direction
blocks.text: tap your hero's arrow to spin which way they start facing

blocks.name: test it
blocks.text: play your own level with blocks. If YOU can't beat it, nobody can!

blocks.name: save
blocks.text: name it and save — it shows up in the Workshop for your family to try

intro.goal: Build a level that's tricky but fair. Then hand the iPad to someone and watch them squirm 😈

recap: You designed levels, not just solved them — that's game design
You had to think like a player AND a builder at the same time
Fair-but-tricky is the hardest thing to get right in any game
Everything you learned still applies: your levels need a path, a goal, and a reason to think
